title: TokenizedShareManager Transfer Causes Unbounded Claim Loop
reason: Duplicate of existing F-005 (same root cause and impact already tracked).

title: SignatureQueue Nonce Allows Non-Sequential Orders
reason: `validateOrder` enforces strict equality `order.nonce == nonces(order.caller)`; higher nonces do not pass.

title: Factory Accepts Arbitrary Implementation Addresses
reason: Proposal is permissionless by design, but acceptance is `onlyOwner`; no privilege bypass or direct protocol-harm exploit from this alone.

title: LidoDepositHook Ignores Return Values From External Calls
reason: Referenced calls (`safeIncreaseAllowance`, `withdraw`, `sendValue`) revert on failure rather than failing silently; claim is technically inaccurate.

title: Oracle SubmitReports Has No Rate Limiting Per Oracle
reason: `submitReports` is role-gated and `_handleReport` enforces per-asset timing (`TooEarly`) for normal report cadence; this is not a meaningful security issue.

title: SignatureQueue Order Price Validation Uses Requested/Ordered Ratio
reason: Not a standalone vulnerability as stated; pricing ratio is the intended validated order price for this flow and no concrete exploit path was shown.

title: Revoked compact calls are not deleted from `compactCalls` mapping
reason: Only stale metadata remains; authorization correctness is governed by `compactCallHashes` set membership. No realistic protocol-level harm.
